assertions.reason: POST /api/auth/login returned 200 and set authjs.session-token, but the user was redirected to /onboarding instead of /dashboard after sign-in.
Using the registered email with an incorrect password showed the generic 'Invalid email or password' message and returned 401.
Using authsess-missing-20260401@example.test returned the same generic 'Invalid email or password' response and 401, preventing user enumeration.

The first five consecutive bad-password submissions for authsess-20260401@example.test all returned 401 with the generic error. The lockout message and 429 appeared only on the following attempt, so lockout was enforced one request late.
evidence: network:POST /api/auth/login -> 401 x6, then 429 on the next attempt
response-body:Account temporarily locked due to too many failed login attempts. Try again in 14 minutes or reset your password.

assertions.reason: After login, the app redirects to /onboarding and onboarding completion fails with POST /onboarding -> 500 (column "ip_address" of relation "audit_logs" does not exist). That prevents reaching the dashboard/logout button, so logout and back-navigation could not be validated through the real UI.
evidence: console-errors:column "ip_address" of relation "audit_logs" does not exist

assertions.reason: While authenticated, reloading the protected /onboarding page kept the user on the same page and the session cookie remained present; no 401s were observed during the reload flow.

After 65 seconds of inactivity, reloading the protected page kept the user authenticated on /onboarding and no session-expired message appeared. The session cookie expiry was 2026-05-01T09:20:49.053859Z (~30 days), so there is no visible inactivity-timeout path.

frictions.description: Because authenticated users are redirected away from /login, I had to clear only the browser session's cookies/localStorage after the authenticated checks to continue the negative login tests in the same required browser session.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser cookies/storage clear on the isolated session only.

blockers.description: Organisation onboarding crashes on POST /onboarding with database error `column "ip_address" of relation "audit_logs" does not exist`, preventing access to the dashboard/logout UI.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried the onboarding step through the real UI once; it returned to step 1 and reproduced the same browser/server error.

notes: Registered and verified authsess-20260401@example.test via the real UI and the shared dev-server email log before testing login/session assertions.
Session-persistence and inactivity checks were performed on the authenticated /onboarding page because the post-login flow does not land on /dashboard for a new account.
